notes: Advisor and skilled orator for King Pyrrhus of Epirus, noted for his persuasive speech and diplomatic skill.

docs.title: Roman History, Book Book 9
docs.author: Cassius Dio
docs.excerpt: Now, as a sensible man, he recognized the folly of the expedition and endeavoured to dissuade Pyrrhus from it. For the latter intended by his prowess to rule the whole earth, whereas Cineas urged him to be satisfied with his own possessions, which were sufficient for
